description:
Read ~/dev/sam/docs/initial-research.md thoroughly. Based on the research recommendations (Section 6), create a detailed implementation plan for the music portfolio website using the recommended Astro + wavesurfer.js + howler.js + Cloudflare stack.

Break the plan into concrete, implementable kanban tasks using kanban_create(). Each task should be specific enough for a developer to execute independently. Cover:

1. Project scaffolding (Astro setup, directory structure, config)

2. Audio player component (sticky bottom bar, wavesurfer.js + howler.js integration, playlist, play across pages)

3. Track/release listing pages (album art grid, grouped by release, streaming platform links)

4. Artist bio/about page (photo, bio text, genre tags, press quotes, contact form)

5. SEO and meta tags (Open Graph, Twitter Cards, MusicRecording structured data)

6. Dark mode, responsive, minimal design

7. Cloudflare Pages + R2 deployment setup

8. Analytics (Umami self-hosted)

Create tasks with appropriate dependencies (scaffolding before components, deployment last). Reference the feature lists in Sections 2 and 6 of the research doc for scope. The working directory for all tasks should be ~/dev/sam. The directory for all documented output shuold be ~/dev/sam/docs

log.outcome: This is a well-crafted specification for a planning-only task. The mission is crystal clear (read research → create 8 kanban tasks → write plan summary), every step has concrete checkboxes with specific file paths and task IDs, and the dependency graph is acyclic and logical. The research document exists and its content aligns with all claims made in the spec.

log.action: Research analysis complete. Extracted actionable recommendations from Sections 2 and 6. Key stack: Astro, wavesurfer.js, howler.js, Cloudflare Pages + R2, Umami analytics. 5 Must-Have features, 4 Should-Have, 5 Nice-to-Have identified.
plan review requested for Step 2 (Create Kanban Tasks)

log.outcome: Step 2 is a well-structured, prescriptive plan that lists exactly 8 kanban tasks (KB-002 through KB-009) with all required fields: goal, deliverables, tech details from the research, and dependency references. The step will achieve its outcomes — the worker simply calls `task_create` 8 times with the provided specifications. The dependency chain is valid and acyclic, and all 5 Must-Have features from the research are covered.

log.action: Created all 8 kanban tasks: KB-002 (Project Scaffolding), KB-003 (Audio Player), KB-004 (Track/Release Listing), KB-005 (Artist Bio/About), KB-006 (SEO & Meta Tags), KB-007 (Dark Mode/Design System), KB-008 (Cloudflare Deployment), KB-009 (Umami Analytics). Dependency chain: KB-002 is root with no deps; KB-003/004/005/007/009 depend on KB-002; KB-006 depends on KB-002+KB-004; KB-008 depends on KB-002+KB-003+KB-006.
plan review requested for Step 3 (Write Plan Summary Document)

log.outcome: Step 3 is straightforward and well-scoped: write a single markdown file (`implementation-plan.md`) that summarizes the 8 kanban tasks already created in Step 2. All 8 tasks (KB-002 through KB-009) exist and have well-structured data in their `task.json` files, so the worker has everything needed to produce an accurate summary. The three checkboxes are concrete and verifiable.

log.action: Verification complete. All 8 tasks (KB-002 through KB-009) confirmed created with valid dependency chain. implementation-plan.md written with ASCII dependency graph and 4-phase implementation order. No circular dependencies detected.
